{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55923","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55923/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55923/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55923/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55923","id":2554628428,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6YRIlM","number":55923,"title":"1.11 release notes don't mention new multidimensional logical indexing feature","user":{"login":"rversteegen","id":1165449,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNjU0NDk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1165449?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/rversteegen","html_url":"https://github.com/rversteegen","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rversteegen/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rversteegen/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rversteegen/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rversteegen/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rversteegen/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rversteegen/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rversteegen/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rversteegen/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rversteegen/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-09-29T01:52:05Z","updated_at":"2024-09-29T01:52:05Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I've been reading the 1.11.0-rc3 manual although I still have 1.10 installed, and came across one example which doesn't work in 1.10. So I looked in the [1.11.0-rc3 Release Notes](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1.11.0-rc3/NEWS/) but there's nothing. I suggest adding it but I'm not sure exactly the extent of what's new.

The example from the [Logical indexing](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1.11.0-rc3/manual/arrays/#Logical-indexing) section which doesn't work in 1.10:
```
julia> x = reshape(1:12, 2, 3, 2)
2×3×2 reshape(::UnitRange{Int64}, 2, 3, 2) with eltype Int64:
[:, :, 1] =
 1  3  5
 2  4  6

[:, :, 2] =
 7   9  11
 8  10  12

julia> x[:, [true false; false true; true false]]
2×3 Matrix{Int64}:
 1  5   9
 2  6  10
```
in v1.10 gives:
```
julia> x[:, [true false; false true; true false]]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access 2×3×2 reshape(::UnitRange{Int64}, 2, 3, 2) with eltype Int64 at index [1:2, 3×2 Matrix{Bool}]
```

The corresponding example in the [1.10 manual](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1.10/manual/arrays/#Logical-indexing) is:
```
julia> x[[false, true, true, false], :]
2×4 Matrix{Int64}:
 2  6  10  14
 3  7  11  15
```

Also, this is very difficult to read example which I spent a long time trying to understand, it would be far easier if it were ` x[[true false false; true true false], :]` instead (AFAIU) so the dimensions don't permute.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55923/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55923/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
